Class TeamsAPI

java.lang.Object
com.skyblockexp.teamsapi.api.TeamsAPI
public final class TeamsAPI extends Object
Static facade and entry point for the Teams API.

This class mirrors the design of Vault's API: it is a thin, dependency-free bridge between team plugins (providers) and plugins that consume team data (consumers). Neither side needs to know about each other's implementation directly.

For providers (team plugins): Register your implementation in onEnable() and unregister it in onDisable():

// onEnable
TeamsAPI.registerProvider(this, new MyTeamsServiceImpl());

// onDisable
TeamsAPI.unregisterProvider(myService);

For consumers (plugins that need team data): Obtain a reference to the service during onEnable() or lazily on demand:

if (!TeamsAPI.isAvailable()) {
    getLogger().warning("No team plugin found — team features disabled.");
    return;
}
TeamsService teams = TeamsAPI.getService();
Optional<Team> team = teams.getPlayerTeam(player.getUniqueId());

This class is intentionally final with a private constructor; it should never be instantiated.

  • Field Details

    • API_VERSION

      public static final String API_VERSION
      The current Teams API version string.

      Providers and consumers can compare this constant at runtime to ensure compatibility when the API introduces breaking changes. The version follows Semantic Versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • getService

      public static TeamsService getService()
      Returns the currently registered TeamsService provider, or null if no provider has been registered.

      The lookup is performed through Bukkit's ServicesManager. If multiple providers are registered, the one with the highest ServicePriority is returned.

      Returns:
      the active TeamsService, or null if unavailable

    • isAvailable

      public static boolean isAvailable()
      Returns true if at least one TeamsService provider is currently registered.

      Consumers should check this before calling getService() to ensure graceful behaviour when no team plugin is installed.

      Returns:
      true if a provider is available, false otherwise

    • registerProvider

      public static void registerProvider(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin, TeamsService provider)
      Registers a TeamsService provider with Bukkit's ServicesManager at ServicePriority.Normal.

      If another provider is already registered at a higher priority, consumers will continue to use that provider. Use ServicePriority.High or ServicePriority.Highest when registering via Bukkit's ServicesManager directly if precedence matters.

      This method silently ignores null arguments.

      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin registering the provider; must not be null
      provider - the TeamsService implementation; must not be null

    • registerProvider

      public static void registerProvider(org.bukkit.plugin.Plugin plugin, TeamsService provider, org.bukkit.plugin.ServicePriority priority)
      Registers a TeamsService provider with Bukkit's ServicesManager at the specified priority.

      This method silently ignores null arguments.

      Parameters:
      plugin - the plugin registering the provider; must not be null
      provider - the TeamsService implementation; must not be null
      priority - the ServicePriority to register at; must not be null

    • unregisterProvider

      public static void unregisterProvider(TeamsService provider)
      Unregisters a TeamsService provider from Bukkit's ServicesManager.

      Providers should call this in their plugin's onDisable() to allow consumers to detect when the service becomes unavailable.

      This method silently ignores a null argument.

      Parameters:
      provider - the TeamsService provider to unregister; may be null
